title: Professor Meeting: Grade Concern
situation: You want to discuss a grade and understand the rubric without sounding confrontational.
userGoal: Ask specific questions and request guidance for improvement.
pressureQuestion: Are you saying my grading was unfair?
avoidSaying: You graded me wrong, fix it now.
Everyone says your class is unfair.
safePhrases: I want to understand how my argument could be stronger.
Could we review one rubric category together?
betterAnswer: I appreciate your feedback. I want to improve, so could we look at the evidence section and what a stronger version would include?

title: Housing Office: File a Complaint Politely
situation: You need to report ongoing maintenance noise and request a formal follow-up.
userGoal: State timeline, impact, and requested next steps in professional English.
pressureQuestion: What outcome are you requesting from our office?
avoidSaying: I'll cause trouble if this continues.
safePhrases: I'd like to file a formal complaint and receive a case number.
The noise has continued from 11 p.m. to 2 a.m. for four nights.
betterAnswer: I'd like to submit a formal complaint about repeated overnight noise in Building C and ask for written follow-up on the next steps.
